seoTitle: Debenham vs Dee Why — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Debenham and Dee Why. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Debenham vs Dee Why: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Debenham and Dee Why.
heading: Debenham vs Dee Why — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Debenham, New South Wales and Dee Why, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Debenham has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Dee Why (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Debenham or Dee Why safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Debenham has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Dee Why scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Debenham scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Dee Why scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Debenham or Dee Why?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Debenham has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
